technology: PWA chat backend: default route to Hermes via openrouter/free
summary: Until OpenClaw's HTTP chat endpoint + Codex OAuth integration are wired, the PWA backend routes all non-@-mentioned messages to Hermes. Hermes uses openrouter/free as its model (auto-routes to a working free OpenRouter model). End-to-end chat round-trip now functional via https://cto.husband.llc/ with the PWA_AUTH_TOKEN.
rationale: Investigation found OpenClaw's HTTP gateway does NOT expose POST /v1/chat/completions or any chat-style endpoint (the gateway runs 6 plugins: browser, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice — none of which is chat). PWA backend's OPENCLAW_CHAT_URL is unreachable.

OpenClaw's per-agent auth profile (~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json) needs an openai-codex entry the install script never wrote. CTO-DECISION-008 (ChatGPT Business via Codex OAuth) is still the architectural target but the wiring is non-trivial — codex CLI's auth.json on disk doesn't auto-flow into OpenClaw's per-agent auth store.

Hermes by contrast routes through OpenRouter natively. Once OPENROUTER_API_KEY landed in ~/.hermes/.env (it had only been in /opt/cto/.env which Hermes doesn't read), Hermes successfully completed a chat round-trip.
openrouter/free auto-routes to a working free model — confirmed end-to-end with 'reply with single word: alive' → 'alive'.

Routing default to Hermes is INTERIM. When OpenClaw chat path + Codex OAuth integration land, default reverts to OpenClaw per the two-hemisphere design (OpenClaw left = thinking/decider, Hermes right = doing/executor).

live_state_on_cto_v1.pwa_backend_public_paths: static assets, manifest.json, service-worker.js, /, /index.html, /api/health bypass auth wall (so PWA shell can bootstrap before JS attaches the Bearer token)
live_state_on_cto_v1.hermes_env_mirror: OPENROUTER_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY mirrored from /opt/cto/.env into ~/.hermes/.env
live_state_on_cto_v1.openclaw_chat: NOT WORKING — no HTTP chat endpoint, codex auth profile missing

remaining_work: OpenClaw codex auth: 2026-05-21 attempt — added profile `openai-codex:default` with full token bundle from ~/.codex/auth.json AND `openai-codex:codex-cli` external bootstrap stub. OpenClaw still rejects with 'No API key found for provider openai-codex'. Source inspection reveals OpenClaw recognizes codex CLI as a RUNTIME (config: `agents.defaults.agentRuntime.id = codex-cli`) where it shells out to `codex` CLI per turn rather than using embedded LLM client. Switching runtime is an architectural change. Alternative: reproduce the interactive `openclaw onboard` ChatGPT-OAuth flow non-interactively (schema undocumented). Either path is hours-level investigation. Pushed to next session.
Find or build an OpenClaw chat HTTP endpoint that the PWA backend can POST to — or switch PWA backend to subprocess `openclaw chat` style
Generate /static/icon-192.png so the PWA manifest reference doesn't 404 (cosmetic; doesn't block install-to-home-screen)
Test SSE stream behaviour from a real browser; current curl tests showed early termination but may be client-side timeout
Add OpenRouter credit monitoring + alerting (free tier rate-limits — chat will silently fail under load)
verified_2026_05_21: POST /api/messages (text="reply with the single word: alive", no @mention) → routed to hermes → returned chat content 'alive'
POST /api/messages (text="@hermes one word: pong") → routed to hermes → returned chat content 'pong'
All static assets serve 200 (style.css, app.js, manifest.json, service-worker.js)

rollback.instructions: Edit services/pwa/backend/server.py: change parse_mention default from 'hermes' back to 'openclaw'. systemctl --user restart cto-pwa-backend. Remove OPENROUTER_API_KEY from ~/.hermes/.env. hermes config set model openai-codex/gpt-5.5.

related_decision_log: CTO-DECISION-008 (ChatGPT Business via Codex OAuth — long-term target; this entry is the interim deviation)
